metadata.notes: Updated evaluators with more comprehensive assertions for Project-based workflow

runs.notes: Project creation, custom fields, labels, and iteration configuration
Without skill guidance, users miss Project setup and custom fields
Adding urgent issues to Sprint, managing Status field, removing blockers
Users struggle with Project field updates and capacity planning
Sprint closure, carryover handling, retrospectives, releases
Basic release creation possible, but Project-based workflow unclear

notes: ANALYST: v2.0 migrated from Milestones to GitHub Projects with Iterations for cleaner, more native workflow.
ANALYST: With-skill responses demonstrate mastery of gh project commands, custom field management, and iteration configuration.
ANALYST: Eval 0 (initialization) validates Project creation with custom fields - 100% pass rate.
ANALYST: Eval 1 (mid-sprint) focuses on Project field updates (Sprint assignment, Status transitions) - 100% pass rate.
ANALYST: Eval 2 (sprint closure) validates iteration closure and carryover handling - 83.3% pass rate.
ANALYST: Minor gap in eval-2: carryover handling with gh project commands needs clearer examples.
ANALYST: Without-skill responses struggle significantly with Project configuration and field management.
ANALYST: Key advantage: Project Iterations provide better UI, automation hooks, and multi-repo support compared to Milestones.
ANALYST: Skill includes automation workflows for teams wanting full CI/CD integration with their Scrum process.
